macro_segments.title: Combining Different Types of Spices and the Principle of Tziruf

micro_segments.title: Minimum amount of spices to be chayav and whether different types combine
Tosafos's discussion on different prohibitions combining and the nature of lav
Chizkiya's principle that spices must be of similar type (sweet spices) to combine
Gemara's question on why similar-type spices are required and comparison to other tziruf cases

macro_segments.title: Dyes and Plant Materials Used for Coloring Garments

micro_segments.title: Shells of nuts and pomegranates and other plants used for dyes with minimum measure
Distinction between raw plant dyes and processed liquid dyes; significance based on processing stage

macro_segments.title: Cleansing Agents: Urine, Natron, Soap, and Related Substances

micro_segments.title: Urine (mei raglayim) and its time-dependent effectiveness; natron types (Alexandrian and other)
Borit identified as sulfur (gafrit); Tosfos's challenge based on shevi'is status and plant root requirement
Borit as a variant of ahala; identification of various other cleaners (ash-lag, kimuliya, etc.)

macro_segments.title: Sacred Items and Geniza: Altar Stones, Books, and Waste Products

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehuda's view that items of idolatry (appurtenances) require destruction regardless of size

macro_segments.title: Fragrances, Oils, and Miscellaneous Items: Pepper, Tar, Incense, and Metals

micro_segments.title: Pilpeles (pepper) and its use to mask bad breath; minimal amounts
Itran (tar) for headache treatment and various fragrances for masking bad smells
Fine oils, purple dye, rose petals, and various metals used for different purposes
Iron and copper vows to the Beit HaMikdash; minimum amounts and uses (spikes, small forks)

macro_segments.title: Sacred Items and Geniza: Altar Stones, Books, and Waste Products (continued)

micro_segments.title: Waste of Sefer Torah and sacred wrappings; obligation to bury even smallest amount
Animals that eat books and other materials; makak, takhakh, ila, and peh identified
Story of student eating figs with peh worm; danger to health

macro_segments.title: Box of Fragrances and Seeds: Combining Multiple Items in a Single Act

micro_segments.title: Taking out box (kuppat) of multiple fragrance compartments as one carrying act; only one sin offering

macro_segments.title: Seeds for Planting and Germination: Minimum Amounts by Type and Purpose

micro_segments.title: Garden seeds (zeronei gina) and their minimum measures; Rabbi Yehuda ben Beseira (five) vs. others
Cucumber, gourd, and Egyptian bean (pul) seeds with measure of two

macro_segments.title: Locusts and Grasshoppers: Kosher and Non-Kosher Species

micro_segments.title: Live kosher grasshopper (chagav chai tahor) vs. dead; kol shehu for live, size of fig for dead
Tziporet keramim (non-kosher vine grasshopper) used for refuah; kol shehu whether alive or dead
Debate between Tanna Kamma and Rabbi Yehuda on live non-kosher locust: significance for children's play

macro_segments.title: Seeds for Planting and Germination: Minimum Amounts by Type and Purpose (continued)

micro_segments.title: Gemara on why seeds for planting require minimum (not taken out singly) vs. seeds for other uses

macro_segments.title: Hair and Plant Fibers: Uses in Traps, Stitching, and Weaving

micro_segments.title: Horse and cow tail hairs (two minimum) for use as snares/traps
Pig hair (one minimum) used for stitching; palm fronds and vines for weaving baskets

macro_segments.title: Locusts and Grasshoppers: Kosher and Non-Kosher Species (continued)

micro_segments.title: Tziporet keramim used for wisdom: consuming half to learn, suspending half on left arm for retention
Story of Rav Kahana and the shushiva locust flying near mouth; concern for appearance and bal teshaktzu
Gemara's exploration: whether concern is alive vs. dead, and the principle of bal teshaktzu (not making oneself disgusting)
Rabbi Yehuda's counter-argument: kids will eulogize dead non-kosher locusts, so parents allow them as pets
Rashi on direct feeding (ma'achil be'yadayim) and its implications for parents giving toys to children

macro_segments.title: The Principle of Matznia (Setting Aside): Intent and Significance in Carrying

micro_segments.title: Ha'matznia: setting aside items for planting, sampling, or healing; chayav on kol shehu (smallest amount)
Normal person is chayav only on standard measures; bringing back item negates earlier significance
Gemara's question: why say 'matznia' (setting aside) when the real factor is intent at time of carrying out
Abaye's resolution: matznia case involves forgetting original reason; still defined by prior intent (kal ha'oseh al da'at rishonah)
Radical implication: any item taken out with specific use-intent becomes chayav kol shehu, even without formal matznia
Tosafos's challenge: if intent matters, why do different seed types have different measures? Resolution via objective use categories

topical_tags.term: Rabbi Yehuda vs. Tanna Kamma (locusts dispute)
Intent and purpose (taking out to use)
Kal ha'oseh al da'at rishonah (defined by original intent)
